s: Spot satellites overhead in augmented reality
d:
Point your phone at the sky and Cosmovisor overlays the satellites
passing over you right now on the camera image, in augmented reality.
Plus pass predictions, ham-radio tools and a star map.

All the orbital math runs on your device. Works fully offline once the
public orbital data has been downloaded.

FEATURES

* AR view: camera preview with satellites drawn at their real sky
  position, refreshed every second. Tap one for its data.
* Satellite list: search by name or NORAD id; filter by category
  (ISS, Starlink, GPS, weather, science...), object type
  (payload / rocket body / debris), country/operator, orbit regime
  (LEO/MEO/GEO/HEO), radio band and "above the horizon now".
* Passes: 24-hour pass predictions grouped by day, with opt-in
  alerts before a pass.
* Sky and astronomy: Sun, Moon and planets, Moon calendar, eclipses,
  a pannable planetarium, Starlink train finder, and bright comets
  and asteroids from JPL's Small-Body Database.
* Ham radio: live full-duplex Doppler (including inverted
  transponders), rig and rotor control over TCP (rigctld/rotctld),
  ISS SSTV and APRS decoded from the phone mic, RTL-SDR reception
  over rtl_tcp (NBFM, SSTV, NOAA APT weather imagery), and a QSO log
  with ADIF export. All opt-in.
* Night mode: a red theme to preserve your dark adaptation.

PRIVACY

Privacy comes first. No accounts, no ads, no tracking, no telemetry.

* Your GPS location is processed only on the device. It is never sent
  or stored off-device.
* The only network requests fetch public orbital data (CelesTrak, and
  optionally the SatNOGS transponder catalog and JPL small-body data).
  They are anonymous and carry no personal data or location.
* Works offline with the last downloaded data.

Cosmovisor is fully de-Googled: no Google Play Services, no Firebase,
no trackers. Location uses the AOSP LocationManager and the fonts are
bundled, so it runs on degoogled devices.

later (GPL-3.0-or-later).
